title: Debt Recovery

statuteRef.label: Time limit for actions founded on simple contract (debt)

questions.label: Date the debt became due
questions.helpText: The date on which the debt became payable. For a loan, this is typically the date of default or when the debt was demanded and not paid. For goods or services, it is when payment was due under the contract.

questions.label: Was the claimant under a disability at the date of accrual?
questions.helpText: Under the Limitation Act 1980, a person is under a disability if they are a minor (under 18) or lack capacity within the meaning of the Mental Capacity Act 2005.

questions.label: Date disability ceased
questions.helpText: The date the claimant turned 18, or the date capacity was regained.

questions.label: Was there fraud, deliberate concealment, or mistake?
questions.helpText: Under s.32 Limitation Act 1980, if the defendant deliberately concealed relevant facts, the limitation period does not begin until the claimant discovered or could with reasonable diligence have discovered the concealment.

questions.label: Date of discovery
questions.helpText: The date the claimant discovered, or could with reasonable diligence have discovered, the fraud, concealment, or mistake.

questions.label: Has the debtor made a written, signed acknowledgment of the debt?
questions.helpText: Under s.29 Limitation Act 1980, a written acknowledgment signed by the debtor (or their agent) causes the limitation period to run afresh from the date of acknowledgment. Common examples include written confirmation of the debt or a signed repayment agreement.

questions.label: Date of acknowledgment
questions.helpText: The date of the written, signed acknowledgment.

questions.label: Has the debtor made a part payment on the debt?
questions.helpText: Under s.29 Limitation Act 1980, a part payment by the debtor (or their agent) causes the limitation period to run afresh from the date of payment.

questions.label: Date of part payment
questions.helpText: The date the most recent part payment was made.

notes: For instalment debts, each missed instalment may give rise to a separate cause of action with its own limitation period.
For debts payable on demand, the cause of action accrues on the date of the loan (not the date of demand), unless the contract provides otherwise.
If the debt is contained in a deed, the 12-year limitation period under s.8 applies instead. Use the Deed Claim (Specialty Contract) calculator.
Part payments and acknowledgments are particularly common in debt recovery and can significantly extend the effective limitation period.
